{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2013 Cannes Film Festival | image = 2013 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 66th Cannes Film Festival featuring a photo of [[Paul Newman]] and [[Joanne Woodward]] during the shooting of the film ''[[A New Kind of Love]]'' | host = [[Audrey Tautou]] | number = 20 (In Competition) | opening = ''[[The Great Gatsby (2013 film)|The Great Gatsby]]'' | closing = ''[[Zulu (2013 film)|Zulu]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Blue Is the Warmest Colour]]'' | date = {{Start date|2013|05|15|df=y}} – {{End date|2013|05|26|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2012 Cannes Film Festival|2012]] | next = [[2014 Cannes Film Festival|2014]] }} The '''66th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 15 to 26 May 2013.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2013.htm |title=66ème Festival de Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |access-date=29 July 2017}} American filmmaker [[Steven Spielberg]] was the Jury President for the main competition.{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-21612509 |title=Steven Spielberg to head up Cannes Film Festival jury |work=BBC News |date=28 February 2013 |access-date=26 May 2017}} The French film ''[[Blue Is the Warmest Colour]]'' won the [[Palme d'Or]]. In an unprecedented move, along with the director, [[Abdellatif Kechiche]], the Jury decided to take "the exceptional step" of awarding the film's two main actresses, [[Adèle Exarchopoulos]] and [[Léa Seydoux]], with the Palme.{{cite web |date=26 May 2013 |title=Blue is the warmest colour team win Palme d'Or at Cannes 2013 |url=http://www.english.rfi.fr/culture/20130526-wins-palme-dor-cannes-2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130608102433/http://www.english.rfi.fr/culture/20130526-wins-palme-dor-cannes-2013 |archive-date=8 June 2013 |access-date=26 May 2013 |publisher=RFI}} The festival poster featured the real-life couple and [[Paul Newman]] and [[Joanne Woodward]] kissing during the shooting of ''[[A New Kind of Love]]''.{{Cite web |last=Kemp |first=Stuart |date=26 March 2013 |title=Festival de Cannes Unveils 2013 Poster |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/festival-de-cannes-unveils-2013-430364 |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |publisher=Prometheus Global Media}} French actress [[Audrey Tautou]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies.{{Cite web |title=Cannes Film Festival 2013: Audrey Tautou to host opening ceremony |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/cannes-film-festival/9981684/Cannes-Film-Festival-2013-Audrey-Tautou-to-host-opening-ceremony.html |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=The Telegraph}} On the occasion of ''100 Years of [[Indian Cinema]]'', India was an Official Guest Country at the 2013 Cannes Film Festival. Seven Indian feature films were premiered among various sections on the festival.{{cite web |url=http://www.ambafrance-in.org/India-the-guest-country-at-the,11203 |title=Cannes Film Festival: India guest country, Vidya Balan on jury, 24 April 2013 |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite web |url=http://www.bollywoodlife.com/south-gossip/ss-rajamoulis-eega-to-be-screened-at-cannes-shanghai-film-festivals/ |title=SS Rajamouli's Eega to be screened at Cannes, Shanghai film festivals |author=IANS |publisher=bollywoodlife.com |date=15 May 2013 |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.vogue.in/content/sonam-vidya-cannes-festival-2013-best-dressed |title=Who Wore What: Indian stars at Cannes 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014140736/http://www.vogue.in/content/sonam-vidya-cannes-festival-2013-best-dressed |archive-date=14 October 2014 }} Actress [[Vidya Balan]] was one of the Jury members for the Main Competition of the festival.{{cite web|url=http://www.thehindu.com/features/cinema/vidya-balan-in-cannes-film-festivals-jury/article4649968.ece |title=Vidya Balan in Cannes Film Festival's jury |date=24 April 2013 |work=The Hindu |access-date=26 May 2017}} The first [[Incredible India]] ''Exhibition'', a joint participation of the [[Ministry of Tourism (India)|Ministry of Tourism]] and [[Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (India)|Ministry of Information and Broadcasting]], [[Republic of India]] was inaugurated by Indian delegate [[Chiranjeevi]].{{cite news |url=https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/entertainment/regional/telugu/news-interviews/Chiranjeevi-to-inaugurate-Incredible-India-exhibition-at-Cannes/articleshow/20151103.cms |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130620151939/http://articles.timesofindia.indiatimes.com/2013-05-20/news-interviews/39391930_1_incredible-india-film-industry-films-and-documentaries |url-status=live |archive-date=20 June 2013 |title=Chiranjeevi to inaugurate Incredible India exhibition at Cannes |date=20 May 2013 |work=[[The Times of India]] |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite news |url=http://www.business-standard.com/article/news-ani/chiranjeevi-offers-wide-opportunities-to-foreign-film-producers-in-india-113052100253_1.html |title=Chiranjeevi offers wide opportunities to foreign film producers in India |last=ANI |work=business-standard.com |date=21 May 2013 |access-date=26 May 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[The Great Gatsby (2013 film)|The Great Gatsby]]'' by [[Baz Luhrmann]],{{cite web |last=Zeitchik |first=Steve |date=12 March 2013 |title=Cannes 2013: Baz Luhrmann's 'The Great Gatsby' to open festival |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-cannes-great-gatsby-opens-20130312,0,3484369.story |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=Los Angeles Times}}{{cite web |last=Geoghegan |first=Kev |date=15 May 2013 |title=Great Gatsby to kick off Cannes Film Festival |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-22526407 |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=BBC News}} and closed with ''[[Zulu (2013 film)|Zulu]]'' by [[Jérôme Salle]].{{cite web |date=12 April 2013 |title='Zulu' to Close Cannes Film Festival |url=https://variety.com/2013/film/international/zulu-to-close-cannes-film-festival-1200353633/ |url-status=dead The following 18 entries (14 fiction films and 4 animation films) were selected, out of 1,550 submissions from 277 different schools. One-third of the films selected represented schools competing for the first time. It was also the first time for a Chilean film to be selected in Cinéfondation.{{Cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2013/cinefondation.html |title=Official Selection 2013: Cinefondation |work=festival-cannes.com |publisher=[[Cannes Film Festival]] |access-date=29 March 2015 |url-status=dead ==Official Awards== [[File:Abdellatif Kechiche Cannes 2013.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Abdellatif Kechiche]], winner of the 2013 Palme d'Or]] [[File:Coen brothers Cannes 2015 2 (CROPPED).jpg|thumb|200px|[[Coen brothers|Joel & Ethan Coen]], winners of the Gran Prix]] [[File:Cannes tent.jpg|thumb|Registration and accreditation tent for the 2013 Festival]]The French film ''[[Blue Is the Warmest Colour]]'', directed by [[Abdellatif Kechiche]], won the [[Palme d'Or]].{{cite news|url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/movies/2013/05/26/cannes-winner/2361731/ |title='Blue is the Warmest Color' wins top prize at Cannes |date=26 May 2013 |work=[[USA Today]] |access-date=26 May 2017}} In a first for the competition, the jury decided to award the Palme d'Or to Kechiche and the actresses who star in the film: [[Adèle Exarchopoulos]] and [[Léa Seydoux]].{{cite news|url=https://variety.com/2013/film/news/cannes-blue-is-the-warmest-color-wins-palme-d-or-1200488202/ |title=Cannes: 'Blue Is the Warmest Color' Wins Palme d' Or |last=Chang |first=Justin |date=26 May 2013 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=26 May 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130607144338/http://variety.com/2013/film/news/cannes-blue-is-the-warmest-color-wins-palme-d-or-1200488202/ |archive-date=7 June 2013 }} ''Blue Is the Warmest Colour'' is a coming-of-age film that tells the story of a lesbian relationship between a 15-year-old girl and an older woman.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-22676798 |title=Cannes Film Festival: Lesbian drama wins Palme d'Or |date=26 May 2013 |work=[[BBC News]] |access-date=26 May 2017}} It has shocked some critics with its graphic and controversial sex scenes.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2013/may/26/cannes-blue-is-the-warmest-colour |title=Cannes 2013 Palme d'Or goes to film about lesbian romance |last=Pulver |first=Andrew |date=26 May 2013 |work=The Guardian |access-date=26 May 2017}} A reporter for the [[Radio France Internationale]] stated that Kechiche paid tribute to the "Tunisian revolution" and "the right to love freely" during his acceptance speech. The president of the jury, [[Steven Spielberg]], said "The film is a great love story ... We were absolutely spellbound by the two brilliant young actresses, and the way the director observed his young players." The [[Grand Prix (Cannes Film Festival)|Grand Prix]] was won by the [[Coen brothers]]'s ''[[Inside Llewyn Davis]]'', while [[Bruce Dern]] and [[Bérénice Bejo]] were awarded [[Best Actor Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actor]] and [[Best Actress Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actress]] respectively. === In Competition === * [[Palme d'Or]]: [[Abdellatif Kechiche]] (filmmaker), [[Adèle Exarchopoulos]] and [[Léa Seydoux]] (lead actresses) for ''[[Blue Is the Warmest Colour]]{{Cite web |title=Awards 2013: All the awards |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2013/allAward.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161122075331/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2013/allAward.html |archive-date=22 November 2016 |work=festival-cannes.com |publisher=Cannes Film Festival}}'' * [[Grand Prix (Cannes Film Festival)|Grand Prix]]: ''[[Inside Llewyn Davis]]'' by [[Coen brothers|Joel & Ethan Coen]] * [[Best Director Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Director]]: [[Amat Escalante]] for ''[[Heli (film)|Heli]]'' * [[Best Screenplay Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Screenplay]]: [[Jia Zhangke]] for ''[[A Touch of Sin]]'' * [[Best Actress Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actress]]: [[Bérénice Bejo]] for ''[[The Past (2013 film)|The Past]]'' * [[Best Actor Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actor]]: [[Bruce Dern]] for ''[[Nebraska (film)|Nebraska]]'' * [[Jury Prize (Cannes Film Festival)|Jury Prize]]: ''[[Like Father, Like Son (2013 film)|Like Father, Like Son]]'' by [[Hirokazu Koreeda]] === ''Un Certain Regard'' |title=Cannes: 'The Missing Picture' Wins Un Certain Regard Prize |date=26 May 2013 |work=Hollywood Reporter |access-date=26 May 2017}} * '''Un Certain Regard Special Jury Prize''': ''[[Omar (2013
